title: State Persistence
concept: How an agent remembers its plan and tool results across loop iterations, preventing state loss.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, how did an agent figure out WHICH tool to call and with what inputs?

options.text: By parsing the model's output (regex, XML tags, or JSON) to extract the action and its parameters
By randomly guessing a tool from the list
By asking the user to type the tool name every time

exercises.explain: Parsing action intents means reading the model's reply and pulling out the tool name and its inputs, using patterns like regex, XML tags, or structured JSON.

cards.title: Wait... what was I doing?
cards.body: An agent loops many times: think, act, observe, repeat. But if it forgets its plan each loop, it starts over forever. State persistence is how it remembers.

cards.title: State Persistence
cards.body: State persistence means saving the agent's plan, past steps, and tool results so they carry over into the next loop. Without it, the agent loses its memory between iterations.

cards.title: Like a detective's notebook
cards.body: A detective writes each clue in a notebook. Next room, they reread it instead of forgetting. The agent's saved state is that notebook: every result gets written down for later.

cards.title: Carrying results forward
cards.body: Loop 1: agent calls a weather tool and gets '18°C'. It saves that. Loop 2: it remembers 18°C and decides to suggest a jacket, instead of asking for the weather again.

cards.title: Myth: the model just remembers
cards.body: The AI model itself is stateless each call. It only 'remembers' because the program feeds the saved history back in. The persistence lives in the agent's code, not the model's brain.

cards.title: Memory vs. Amnesia
cards.body: Persisting state keeps progress. Losing state means starting from zero every loop.

left.text: Remembers plan + past tool results, builds on them each loop

right.text: Forgets everything, repeats the same steps forever

exercises.prompt: Why does an agent need state persistence across loop iterations?

options.text: So it remembers its plan and earlier tool results instead of starting over each loop
So it can run without any tools
So the model becomes faster at math
So the user never has to see the answer

exercises.explain: Each loop builds on the last. Persisting the plan and past results lets the agent make progress instead of forgetting and repeating itself.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The AI model automatically remembers past loops on its own, so the agent's code doesn't need to save anything.
exercises.explain: False. The model is stateless per call. The agent's code must save the history and feed it back in, or the memory is lost.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about carrying information between loops.
exercises.sentence: Saving the plan and tool results so they carry into the next loop is called state persistence.
exercises.explain: State persistence keeps the agent's progress alive across iterations, so it doesn't lose what it already learned.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of state persistence sounds AI-generated (smooth and textbook-perfect)?

options.text: State persistence enables an agent to retain its plan and prior tool outputs across successive iterations, thereby maintaining continuity and preventing redundant recomputation.
it saves the stuff so next loop it don't forget lol
Basically the agent keeps notes so it doesn't redo work.

exercises.explain: Option A is the polished, evenly-structured wording typical of AI text. The others are casual and human-sounding.

exercises.prompt: An agent looks up a flight price (Loop 1), then needs to compare it to a train price (Loop 2). In Loop 2 it re-searches the flight instead of comparing. What broke, and what fixes it?

options.text: State wasn't persisted, so the flight result was lost. Saving tool results into the agent's memory and feeding them back fixes it.
The model was too slow. A faster model fixes it.
The parsing failed. Better regex fixes it.
There were too many tools. Removing tools fixes it.

exercises.explain: The agent forgot Loop 1's flight result because that state wasn't saved and passed forward. State persistence stores each result so later loops can build on it instead of repeating work. That memory lives in the agent's code, not the stateless model.
